parts.text: Inspect one or two existing tool implementations (prefer run_command.go and read_file.go) and return exact pattern for ToolEntry, Definition, Execute, deferred toolVerboseLog usage, and summary string style. Return concise snippets and file:line references only.

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/run_command.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"errors"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"os/exec"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
11: )
12: 
13: // truncateCommand truncates a command string to maxLen chars, appending "..." if longer.
14: func truncateCommand(cmd string, maxLen int) string {
15: 	if len(cmd) <= maxLen {
16: 		return cmd
17: 	}
18: 	return cmd[:maxLen] + "..."
19: }
20: 
21: const maxOutputBytes = 100 * 1024 // 102400 bytes
22: 
23: // runCommandEntry returns the ToolEntry for the run_command tool.
24: func runCommandEntry() ToolEntry {
25: 	return ToolEntry{
26: 		Definition: runCommandDefinition,
27: 		Execute:    runCommandExecute,
28: 	}
29: }
30: 
31: func runCommandDefinition() provider.Tool {
32: 	return provider.Tool{
33: 		Name:        toolname.RunCommand,
34: 		Description: "Execute a shell command in the agent's working directory via sh -c and return the combined stdout/stderr output.",
35: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
36: 			"command": {
37: 				Type:        "string",
38: 				Description: "The shell command to execute.",
39: 				Required:    true,
40: 			},
41: 		},
42: 	}
43: }
44: 
45: func runCommandExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
46: 	command := call.Arguments["command"]
47: 	truncCmd := truncateCommand(command, 60)
48: 	var cmdErr error
49: 
50: 	defer func() {
51: 		var summary string
52: 		if result.IsError {
53: 			var exitErr *exec.ExitError
54: 			if errors.As(cmdErr, &exitErr) {
55: 				summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (exit %d)", truncCmd, exitErr.ExitCode())
56: 			} else if cmdErr != nil {
57: 				summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (%s)", truncCmd, cmdErr.Error())
58: 			} else {
59: 				summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (%s)", truncCmd, result.Content)
60: 			}
61: 		} else {
62: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("%q (exit 0)", truncCmd)
63: 		}
64: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.RunCommand, result, summary)
65: 	}()
66: 
67: 	if command == "" {
68: 		return provider.ToolResult{
69: 			CallID:  call.ID,
70: 			Content: "command is required",
71: 			IsError: true,
72: 		}
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sh", "-c", command)
76: 	cmd.Dir = ec.Workdir
77: 
78: 	output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
79: 
80: 	// Truncate output if it exceeds 100KB.
81: 	outStr := string(output)
82: 	if len(output) > maxOutputBytes {
83: 		outStr = string(output[:maxOutputBytes]) + "\n... [output truncated, exceeded 100KB]"
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	if err == nil {
87: 		return provider.ToolResult{
88: 			CallID:  call.ID,
89: 			Content: outStr,
90: 			IsError: false,
91: 		}
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	cmdErr = err
95: 
96: 	var exitErr *exec.ExitError
97: 	if errors.As(err, &exitErr) {
98: 		return provider.ToolResult{
99: 			CallID:  call.ID,
100: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("exit code %d\n%s", exitErr.ExitCode(), outStr),
101: 			IsError: true,
102: 		}
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	return provider.ToolResult{
106: 		CallID:  call.ID,
107: 		Content: err.Error(),
108: 		IsError: true,
109: 	}
110: }

(End of file - total 110 lines)
</content>

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/read_file.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bytes"
5: 	"context"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"os"
8: 	"strconv"
9: 	"strings"
10: 
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
13: )
14: 
15: // readFileEntry returns the ToolEntry for the read_file tool.
16: func readFileEntry() ToolEntry {
17: 	return ToolEntry{
18: 		Definition: readFileDefinition,
19: 		Execute:    readFileExecute,
20: 	}
21: }
22: 
23: func readFileDefinition() provider.Tool {
24: 	return provider.Tool{
25: 		Name:        toolname.ReadFile,
26: 		Description: "Read the contents of a file relative to the working directory. Returns line-numbered output in the format 'N: content' for each line.",
27: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
28: 			"path": {
29: 				Type:        "string",
30: 				Description: "Relative path to the file to read.",
31: 				Required:    true,
32: 			},
33: 			"offset": {
34: 				Type:        "string",
35: 				Description: "1-indexed line number to start reading from. Defaults to 1.",
36: 				Required:    false,
37: 			},
38: 			"limit": {
39: 				Type:        "string",
40: 				Description: "Maximum number of lines to return. Defaults to 2000.",
41: 				Required:    false,
42: 			},
43: 		},
44: 	}
45: }
46: 
47: func readFileExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
48: 	path := call.Arguments["path"]
49: 
50: 	defer func() {
51: 		var summary string
52: 		if result.IsError {
53: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q: %s", path, result.Content)
54: 		} else {
55: 			lineCount := 0
56: 			if result.Content != "" {
57: 				lineCount = strings.Count(result.Content, "\n") + 1
58: 			}
59: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("path %q (%d lines)", path, lineCount)
60: 		}
61: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.ReadFile, result, summary)
62: 	}()
63: 
64: 	resolved, err := validatePath(ec.Workdir, path)
65: 	if err != nil {
66: 		return provider.ToolResult{
67: 			CallID:  call.ID,
68: 			Content: err.Error(),
69: 			IsError: true,
70: 		}
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	info, err := os.Stat(resolved)
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		return provider.ToolResult{
76: 			CallID:  call.ID,
77: 			Content: err.Error(),
78: 			IsError: true,
79: 		}
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	if info.IsDir() {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{
84: 			CallID:  call.ID,
85: 			Content: "path is a directory, not a file",
86: 			IsError: true,
87: 		}
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	content, err := os.ReadFile(resolved)
91: 	if err != nil {
92: 		return provider.ToolResult{
93: 			CallID:  call.ID,
94: 			Content: err.Error(),
95: 			IsError: true,
96: 		}
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	// Binary detection: scan first 512 bytes for NUL byte.
100: 	peek := content
101: 	if len(peek) > 512 {
102: 		peek = peek[:512]
103: 	}
104: 	if bytes.ContainsRune(peek, '\x00') {
105: 		return provider.ToolResult{
106: 			CallID:  call.ID,
107: 			Content: "binary file detected",
108: 			IsError: true,
109: 		}
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	// Parse offset (default 1).
113: 	offset := 1
114: 	if v := call.Arguments["offset"]; v != "" {
115: 		offset, err = strconv.Atoi(v)
116: 		if err != nil {
117: 			return provider.ToolResult{
118: 				CallID:  call.ID,
119: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid offset %q: %s", v, err.Error()),
120: 				IsError: true,
121: 			}
122: 		}
123: 		if offset < 1 {
124: 			return provider.ToolResult{
125: 				CallID:  call.ID,
126: 				Content: "offset must be >= 1",
127: 				IsError: true,
128: 			}
129: 		}
130: 	}
131: 
132: 	// Parse limit (default 2000).
133: 	limit := 2000
134: 	if v := call.Arguments["limit"]; v != "" {
135: 		limit, err = strconv.Atoi(v)
136: 		if err != nil {
137: 			return provider.ToolResult{
138: 				CallID:  call.ID,
139: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("invalid limit %q: %s", v, err.Error()),
140: 				IsError: true,
141: 			}
142: 		}
143: 		if limit < 1 {
144: 			return provider.ToolResult{
145: 				CallID:  call.ID,
146: 				Content: "limit must be >= 1",
147: 				IsError: true,
148: 			}
149: 		}
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	// Empty file early return.
153: 	if len(content) == 0 {
154: 		if offset > 1 {
155: 			return provider.ToolResult{
156: 				CallID:  call.ID,
157: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("offset %d exceeds file length of 0 lines", offset),
158: 				IsError: true,
159: 			}
160: 		}
161: 		return provider.ToolResult{
162: 			CallID:  call.ID,
163: 			Content: "",
164: 			IsError: false,
165: 		}
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	// Split into lines and handle trailing newline.
169: 	lines := strings.Split(string(content), "\n")
170: 	if len(content) > 0 && content[len(content)-1] == '\n' {
171: 		lines = lines[:len(lines)-1]
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	totalLines := len(lines)
175: 
176: 	// Offset bounds check.
177: 	if offset > totalLines {
178: 		return provider.ToolResult{
179: 			CallID:  call.ID,
180: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("offset %d exceeds file length of %d lines", offset, totalLines),
181: 			IsError: true,
182: 		}
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	// Line selection.
186: 	start := offset - 1
187: 	end := start + limit
188: 	if end > totalLines {
189: 		end = totalLines
190: 	}
191: 	selected := lines[start:end]
192: 
193: 	// Output formatting.
194: 	var b strings.Builder
195: 	for i, line := range selected {
196: 		if i > 0 {
197: 			b.WriteByte('\n')
198: 		}
199: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "%d: %s", start+i+1, line)
200: 	}
201: 
202: 	return provider.ToolResult{
203: 		CallID:  call.ID,
204: 		Content: b.String(),
205: 		IsError: false,
206: 	}
207: }

(End of file - total 207 lines)
</content>

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
10: )
11: 
12: // ExecContext holds the minimal context needed by generic tool executors.
13: type ExecContext struct {
14: 	Workdir string
15: 	Stderr  io.Writer
16: 	Verbose bool
17: }
18: 
19: // ToolEntry holds a tool's definition and executor functions.
20: type ToolEntry struct {
21: 	Definition func() provider.Tool
22: 	Execute    func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult
23: }
24: 
25: // Registry maps tool names to their definitions and executors.
26: // Registration must happen at startup before any concurrent Dispatch calls.
27: // Concurrent Dispatch calls (read-only) are safe after registration is complete.
28: type Registry struct {
29: 	entries map[string]ToolEntry
30: }
31: 
32: // NewRegistry returns a new Registry with an empty, initialized entries map.
33: func NewRegistry() *Registry {
34: 	return &Registry{
35: 		entries: make(map[string]ToolEntry),
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: // Register adds a tool entry to the registry, keyed by name.
40: // If a tool with the same name already exists, it is silently replaced.
41: func (r *Registry) Register(name string, entry ToolEntry) {
42: 	r.entries[name] = entry
43: }
44: 
45: // Has returns true if a tool with the given name exists in the registry.
46: func (r *Registry) Has(name string) bool {
47: 	_, ok := r.entries[name]
48: 	return ok
49: }
50: 
51: // Resolve takes a list of tool names and returns their provider.Tool definitions.
52: // Each entry's Definition() is called on every Resolve invocation (not cached).
53: // Returns an error if any name is not found in the registry or has a nil Definition.
54: // Returns an empty non-nil slice for nil or empty input.
55: func (r *Registry) Resolve(names []string) ([]provider.Tool, error) {
56: 	if len(names) == 0 {
57: 		return []provider.Tool{}, nil
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	tools := make([]provider.Tool, 0, len(names))
61: 	for _, name := range names {
62: 		entry, ok := r.entries[name]
63: 		if !ok {
64: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", name)
65: 		}
66: 		if entry.Definition == nil {
67: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no definition", name)
68: 		}
69: 		tools = append(tools, entry.Definition())
70: 	}
71: 	return tools, nil
72: }
73: 
74: // Dispatch looks up the tool by call.Name and executes it.
75: // Returns (ToolResult, nil) on success, or (zero ToolResult, error) for
76: // unknown tools or tools with nil executors.
77: func (r *Registry) Dispatch(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (provider.ToolResult, error) {
78: 	entry, ok := r.entries[call.Name]
79: 	if !ok {
80: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", call.Name)
81: 	}
82: 	if entry.Execute == nil {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no executor", call.Name)
84: 	}
85: 	return entry.Execute(ctx, call, ec), nil
86: }
87: 
88: // RegisterAll registers all implemented tool entries into the given registry.
89: // Both cmd/run.go and ExecuteCallAgent call this after NewRegistry().
90: // Future milestones add lines here — no call-site changes needed.
91: // Safe to call multiple times (Register silently replaces duplicates).
92: func RegisterAll(r *Registry) {
93: 	r.Register(toolname.ListDirectory, listDirectoryEntry())
94: 	r.Register(toolname.ReadFile, readFileEntry())
95: 	r.Register(toolname.WriteFile, writeFileEntry())
96: 	r.Register(toolname.EditFile, editFileEntry())
97: 	r.Register(toolname.RunCommand, runCommandEntry())
98: }

(End of file - total 98 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/verbose.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 
6: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
7: )
8: 
9: // toolVerboseLog emits a single log line to ec.Stderr when ec.Verbose is true.
10: // Format: [tool] <toolName>: <summary> (success|error)
11: func toolVerboseLog(ec ExecContext, toolName string, result provider.ToolResult, summary string) {
12: 	if !ec.Verbose || ec.Stderr == nil {
13: 		return
14: 	}
15: 
16: 	status := "success"
17: 	if result.IsError {
18: 		status = "error"
19: 	}
20: 
21: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(ec.Stderr, "[tool] %s: %s (%s)\n", toolName, summary, status)
22: }

(End of file - total 22 lines)
</content>
